title: Create a TTL index for automatic expiry
description: Use a TTL index on a date field so MongoDB removes expired documents in the background without a cron job.
body: Use when documents should expire automatically (sessions, tokens, ephemeral events). Actions: create an index on a BSON date field with expireAfterSeconds so a background task deletes documents once the field's time plus the interval has passed; for a fixed wall-clock expiry, set the date field to the target time and expireAfterSeconds to 0. Verify by inserting a soon-to-expire document and confirming it disappears after the next sweep. Validation signals include the document being gone within roughly a minute of expiry and the TTLMonitor metric advancing. Risk boundary: the TTL sweep runs about once a minute and is best-effort, so do not rely on it for precise timing or for hard security cutoffs that need immediate deletion.
